tagline: The last backpack you will ever buy.
elevator_pitch: LastPack is a modular backpack system that lets you build your perfect daily carry. Choose your outer frame style, then snap in only the internal modules you actually need, from a lunch compartment to a sunglasses sleeve to reinforced laptop protection.
problem: Backpacks today fall into three frustrating categories: overbuilt hiking packs stuffed with compartments you will never use, overpriced leather briefcase-bags that try too hard, or cheap generic bags that do not try at all. Working professionals carry a backpack every single day, yet no brand designs specifically for the daily commuter who just wants their essentials organized without the clutter.
solution: LastPack offers a modular grid system inside a selection of stylish outer frames. You pick the exterior that matches your aesthetic, then snap in individual modules (lunch, sunglasses, camera, water bottle, charger, keys) onto a standardized internal rail. Every module clicks in and out, so your backpack evolves with your life instead of collecting dust.
target_market: Working professionals aged 22 to 45 who commute daily with a backpack, are frustrated by generic or overengineered options, and are willing to invest in a quality everyday carry solution.
revenue_model: Direct-to-consumer e-commerce with a base frame priced at $129 to $189 depending on style. Individual modules range from $15 to $45 each. Starter bundles (frame plus 4 to 6 modules) offered at $199 to $279. Recurring revenue from seasonal module drops and limited-edition frame collaborations.
key_features: Modular grid system with snap-in compartments you configure yourself
Multiple outer frame styles from minimal canvas to premium leather
Swappable straps with comfort and weight options
Universal laptop sleeve built into every frame
competitive_advantage: While competitors like Away and Timbuk2 offer surface-level customization (colors, flaps), LastPack is the only system where the internal architecture is fully reconfigurable. The standardized grid rail creates a platform ecosystem where new modules can launch continuously, driving repeat purchases and deep brand loyalty.
market_size_estimate: The global backpack market is valued at approximately $24 billion, with the commuter and business segment growing at 6% annually. Targeting the U.S. professional commuter niche alone represents a $2 to $3 billion addressable market.
go_to_market: Launch on Kickstarter with three signature frame styles and a library of 12 initial modules. Target productivity-focused YouTube creators and commuter lifestyle bloggers for early buzz. Follow up with a DTC Shopify store and pop-up events in co-working spaces in major metro areas.
risks: Engineering a durable yet lightweight modular rail system at a consumer-friendly price point
High competition and crowdfunding fatigue in the backpack category on Kickstarter
Educating buyers on the modular concept when most people impulse-buy backpacks
year_one_milestones: Raise $250K+ on Kickstarter and deliver first 2,000 units within 6 months
Launch DTC store with 3 frame styles and 15 module SKUs
Secure placement in 2 to 3 retail partners (e.g., REI, The Container Store)
